_template: Parent-skill mode-registry.json scaffold. Replace every [bracketed] placeholder. Keep one modes[] array for workflow, surface, and transport packets; packetKind is the axis discriminator. The canonical live example is sk-code/mode-registry.json.
description: [Declarative packet registry: workflowMode, packetKind, backendKind, toolSurface, and advisorRouting for [parent-skill-name]. The advisor routes the hub identity; the hub selects workflow and surface packets.]

discriminator.workflowMode: Public hub/mode key. Stable identity used by the hub router and this registry. Carried by every mode.
discriminator.packetKind: Axis discriminator: workflow for process/lifecycle packets, surface for read-only evidence-base packets, transport for packets that bridge to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface.
discriminator.backendKind: Which backend runs the mode. Surface packets use evidence-base. Workflow packets use the hub's declared backend kinds.

advisorRoutingContract.routingClass: How the advisor finds the packet. lexical = weighted-regex scoring and projection maps. alias-fold = projection-only alias. metadata = resolved by hub membership with no advisor map entry. command-bridge = routed by command. Surface packets use metadata.
advisorRoutingContract.legacyAdvisorId: Legacy skill id used by lexical and alias-fold projection maps only.
advisorRoutingContract.advisorDefaultMode: Marks the default mode a multi-mode legacy id folds to. Never inferred from array order.
advisorRoutingContract.legacyAliases: Advisor system-id aliases for lexical or alias-fold routes; drift guards compare these order-insensitively.
advisorRoutingContract.driftGuard: Path to the registry-vs-advisor drift-guard test. Required (here or in the advisor-projection extension) when any mode uses routingClass lexical or alias-fold.
advisorRoutingContract.packetSkillName: The packet SKILL.md name. Folder equals packetSkillName unless grandfatheredFolderMismatch is true.
advisorRoutingContract.grandfatheredFolderMismatch: Required on every mode; false unless a legacy folder/name mismatch is intentionally preserved.
advisorRoutingContract.toolSurface: Required on every mode; declares allowed tools, forbidden tools, mutating posture, and any bash allowlist.

modes._example: routingClass lexical: weighted-regex scored and present in projection maps.
routingClass alias-fold: a legacy id folding to its default mode; projected but not lexically scored.
routingClass metadata: resolved by hub membership; no advisor map entry of its own.
routingClass command-bridge: routed by its command, not by an advisor map entry.
packetKind surface: hub-prefixed read-only evidence base; advisor-invisible metadata route.

packetKind transport: bridges to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface; metadata-routed, non-mutating in this repo, command:null (reached only by pairing with a workflow mode). Keep only under the transport-axis extension and list it there.

extensions._template: OPTIONAL declaration-only block. Keep only extensions this hub actually uses; delete this block for a pure two-tier hub.

runtime-loop._example: Activates runtimeLoopType per workflow mode; lists the valid loop types (runtimeLoopType is validated against exactly these).

advisor-projection._example: Required when any mode uses routingClass lexical or alias-fold.

transform-verbs._example: Declares verb-transform routing; names the top-level block that carries the verb map (the block stays top-level, not inside this extension).

deprecated-modes._example: Declares retired modes kept as guidance; the list lives in the top-level deprecatedModes[] array (blessed top-level location), not inside this block.

surface-axis._example: Declares that this hub carries packetKind surface packets in modes[].

transport-axis._example: Declares packetKind transport packets that bridge to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface; every listed transport must be a registered packetKind:"transport" mode.
